<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- These files are runtime artifacts, not source code — they should remain untracked or carefully versioned depending on team workflow preferences.
- The execution log splitting into numbered files (execution-log.1.jsonl, execution-log.jsonl) suggests log rotation is in place, which is good for long-lived projects.
- Cohesion of 1.0 and zero coupling are expected since this is a data directory with no import relationships.
- Runtime state files like prd.json and execution logs are correctly isolated in .rex/ with no cross-zone import coupling.
- pending-proposals.json and acknowledged-findings.json indicate a review workflow is active; ensure these are committed or gitignored consistently across team members.
- This zone's cohesion=1.0 is computed from directory co-location (all files share .rex/), not from import edges — it is invisible to the Louvain algorithm and would cluster identically regardless of content changes.
- Because this zone has no import edges, any future source file accidentally placed in .rex/ would be silently absorbed into this zone rather than correctly attributed to its logical domain zone.
- Runtime state zone is import-edge-invisible; sourcevision's zone detection for this cluster relies entirely on filesystem proximity rather than structural coupling signals.
- The log rotation naming convention uses a numeric infix (execution-log.1.jsonl) rather than a suffix or timestamp, which means lexicographic sort will not correctly order rotated logs as the rotation index grows beyond single digits (execution-log.9.jsonl sorts after execution-log.10.jsonl).
- Log rotation naming (execution-log.1.jsonl) uses a numeric infix that breaks lexicographic ordering for rotation indices ≥ 10. Switch to zero-padded indices or ISO-date suffixes (execution-log.2026-03-03.jsonl) to ensure correct sort order as logs accumulate.

</insights>
